untranslatable_reason: Signal requires pattern recognition for head-and-shoulders (shoulder prices A, B, D, E; trough prices; neckline geometry with slope measurement in degrees; vertical symmetry validation across multiple price points). These require multi-bar pattern detection, geometric calculations (slope in degrees), and reference point tracking that cannot be expressed using only point-in-time indicator comparisons with allowed operators. The signal needs custom pattern-detection logic beyond the scope of simple indicator thresholds.
translation_notes: ARONSON_DRY_0_2 is a geometric pattern validation rule that requires: (1) identification of 5 reference points (A, B, D, E shoulders/troughs; neckline), (2) calculation of proximity percentages relative to midpoints X and Y, (3) measurement of neckline slope angle in degrees, and (4) symmetry validation logic. None of these can be constructed from the allowed indicators and operators. Pattern-based signals of this type require a dedicated pattern-detection module in the backtester, not parameterized indicator rules.
